genes.name: Atomic independence
genes.meaning: Reusable skill instructions stay self-contained and avoid hard-coded sibling skills, concrete projects, private repositories, or stack mirrors.
genes.failureMode: The skill becomes brittle glue for one local stack and cannot travel cleanly to another context.

genes.name: Portability lens
genes.meaning: The skill separates transportable mechanism from local product, project, or stack-specific context before evolving instructions.
genes.failureMode: Local lessons are mirrored into reusable text and silently narrow the skill's audience.

genes.name: Observation over score
genes.meaning: Metrics and recommendations are treated as signals for agent judgment, not targets to maximize.
genes.failureMode: The ecosystem optimizes dashboards and checklists instead of meaningful skill evolution.

detect.args: Fitness as Signal|observation|not optimization|not an optimization target|score.*signal

genes.name: Progressive disclosure
genes.meaning: The skill starts with the smallest useful contract and adds ceremony only when discovered constraints earn it.
genes.failureMode: The skill becomes overdesigned, hard to activate, and slower than the work it was meant to improve.

genes.name: Graceful degradation
genes.meaning: The skill defines how to narrow, defer, stop, or degrade without leaving dead ceremony or false success.
genes.failureMode: When constraints appear, the skill either keeps pretending to work or collapses without preserving useful boundaries.

genes.name: Context-scoped naming
genes.meaning: Names avoid redundant domain prefixes when directory, package, or protocol context already makes ownership clear.
genes.failureMode: The skill multiplies entities, smears domain boundaries, and makes local artifacts look globally distinct when they are only context-local.

genes.name: Portable standards
genes.meaning: Repeated practice is codified as a transportable standard when it can serve multiple projects or skills; skills are treated as projects when they own protocols, docs, artifacts, or release discipline.
genes.failureMode: Useful lessons stay implicit, local, and non-transferable, so each project or skill rediscovers the same operating rules from scratch.

genes.name: Meta-protocoling
genes.meaning: The skill uses explicit meta-comments or meta-protocols to name the process behind the process: why a rule exists, when it applies, and how it should evolve or degrade.
genes.failureMode: Agents follow rituals without understanding intent, so protocols drift into dead ceremony, hidden coupling, or unreviewable habits.

detect.args: meta-protocol|meta protocol|meta-comment|meta comment|process behind the process|why.*rule exists|rule.*evolve

genes.name: Gene-meme equivalence
genes.meaning: The system treats a gene as a transmissible meme: standards, protocols, and practices become genes only when they carry meaning, change behavior, survive transport, and expose an absent failure mode.
genes.failureMode: The registry confuses local conventions or checklist features for true evolutionary material, so transfer produces ceremony instead of transmissible capability.

genes.name: Memetic recombination
genes.meaning: Any gene-meme may be decomposed into smaller meaning-bearing parts, recombined with other genes, or composed into higher-order protocols to produce emergent properties and new gene-memes.
genes.failureMode: Genes are treated as static labels, so the ecosystem can only copy or delete them instead of discovering new capabilities through composition and recombination.

genes.name: Horizontal gene transfer mode
genes.meaning: Soft proactivity can enter a bounded submode that looks for one valuable gene-meme transfer from a donor skill or standard into one recipient skill without forcing broad propagation.
genes.failureMode: Useful gene-memes remain isolated in their origin context, or the opposite failure occurs: broad propagation copies them into unrelated skills as score-farming ceremony.

detect.args: Horizontal Gene Transfer Mode|horizontal gene transfer mode|bounded gene transfer|donor skill|recipient skill|one valuable gene
